2606.08497.title: Explaining Black-Box Language Models: Learning to Optimize Linguistically-Structured Word Subsets
2606.08497.paper_summary: As deep language models (DLMs) are increasingly deployed in high-stakes domains such as healthcare, understanding their decision rationale becomes paramount for ensuring trust, safety, and accountability. However, achieving this vital level of interpretability is particularly challenging when these DLMs operate as black-box systems (e.g., via APIs), where access to internal model states (e.g., parameters, gradients) is restricted. Despite numerous efforts, existing explanation methods often fail to concurrently satisfy three key desiderata: (i) inference-time efficiency, (ii) black-box compatibility without inducing out-of-distribution behavior, and (iii) comprehensible explanations grounded in the input's linguistic structure. To address these challenges, we propose a method that explains predictions of DLMs by selecting a small, informative subset of input words. We formulate this as an amortized optimization problem, enabling efficient one-shot inference without the need for input-specific search. Our selection policy is trained via REINFORCE-style policy gradients, allowing discrete word selection in a fully gradient-free setting. To enhance interpretability and align with human linguistic intuition, we integrate graph-structured knowledge into this selection process, fostering linguistically coherent subsets that result in explanations both highly informative and cognitively meaningful to end-users. We evaluated our method on diverse DLM architectures and multiple real-world datasets. It consistently identifies word subsets with enhanced discriminative power and stronger alignment with linguistically salient cues, outperforming both conventional black-box compatible methods and gradient-based approaches that are given oracle access to the black-box model's gradients for a more challenging benchmark. Our code is available at here.
2606.08497.paper_summary_zh: 隨著深度語言模型（DLMs）在醫療等高風險領域的日益應用，理解其決策理由對於確保信任、安全和問責至關重要。<lb/>然而，當這些 DLMs 作為黑箱系統運作時（例如，通過 API），實現這一重要的可解釋性水平尤其具有挑戰性，因為對內部模型狀態（例如，參數、梯度）的訪問受到限制。<lb/>儘管已有許多努力，現有的解釋方法往往無法同時滿足三個關鍵需求：（i）推理時的效率，（ii）黑箱兼容性而不引起分佈外行為，以及（iii）基於輸入語言結構的可理解解釋。<lb/>為了解決這些挑戰，我們提出了一種通過選擇一小部分信息豐富的輸入單詞來解釋 DLMs 預測的方法。<lb/>我們將這一問題表述為一個攤銷優化問題，使得在不需要特定於輸入的搜索的情況下能夠高效進行一次性推理。<lb/>我們的選擇策略通過 REINFORCE 風格的策略梯度進行訓練，允許在完全無梯度的設置中進行離散單詞選擇。<lb/>為了增強可解釋性並與人類語言直覺對齊，我們將圖結構知識整合到這一選擇過程中，促進語言上連貫的子集，從而產生對最終用戶既高度信息豐富又認知上有意義的解釋。<lb/>我們在多種 DLM 架構和多個現實世界數據集上評估了我們的方法。<lb/>它始終能識別具有增強區分能力和與語言上顯著線索更強對齊的單詞子集，超越了傳統的黑箱兼容方法和在更具挑戰性的基準中獲得黑箱模型梯度的梯度基方法。我們的代碼可在此獲得。
2606.08497.author: Minyoung Hwang et.al.
2606.08497.authors: Minyoung Hwang, Seokhyun Lee, Changhee Lee
